commit_scope: Execute the Form-native ELF64/aarch64 executable on an authorized Android arm64 adb device and record stdout/stderr/exit proof.

commands: coord join && coord claim "form-native Android adb executable receipt"
adb shell getprop ro.product.model; adb shell getprop ro.product.cpu.abi; adb shell getprop ro.build.version.release
local_validation.notes: An authorized Samsung SM-S918U1 Android 16 arm64-v8a device was available through adb. Focused Form validation returned 63 with `fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way` and `1 ok, 0 divergent`. The metal-plane audit writes the Form-emitted ELF64/aarch64 ET_EXEC image, file(1) reports an ARM aarch64 statically linked executable, pushes it to /data/local/tmp, chmods it, executes it through adb shell, and validates stdout FORM ELF, empty stderr, and exit 42. The same audit still proves Form-native JIT lanes, Mach-O/ELF object floors, form-elf-exec, lowered residuals, oracle learning, and clang-oracle macOS/Android object receipts through fkwu-covered gates.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

deploy_validation.notes: No public deploy performed for this local Android device runner receipt slice.

phase_gate.reason: Local Android adb runner proof passed; CI, review, merge, and deploy remain pending.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: The metal-plane audit now treats an authorized Android arm64 adb device as a first-class executable runner before qemu/Docker fallback. It executes the Form-native ELF64/aarch64 image on the target device and validates stdout FORM ELF, empty stderr, and exit 42.
test_flows: Detect one authorized adb device or a requested FORM_ELF_EXEC_ADB_SERIAL.
Verify the adb device ABI is arm64.
Emit the Form-native ELF executable bytes from form-elf-exec.
Inspect the executable shape with file(1).
Push the executable to /data/local/tmp, run it through adb shell, and require stdout FORM ELF, empty stderr, and exit 42.
